content.title: Map Tools/Options
description: This section provides access to map tools and additional options available on the interactive map interface.
In the Projects section Navigate to the desired Project.
Click on the relevant Workspace
You will now see the interactive map dashboard zoomed in to the set AOI.
Drag the mouse on the map to pan to required location
From Map options on right side –

subdescription: Used to change the base map. Click the Basemap icon and select the desired basemap from the list.
Used to view and manage layers created in the project.
Click the Layer View icon to open the layers panel, where layers are organized by category.
Each layer can be shown or hidden using the eye icon.
Additional options such as Zoom to Layer, Layer Style, Toggle Grid, Delete, and Comment are available through the three-dot menu.
Used to expand the map to full-screen view for better visualization.
Used to measure distance on the map. Select the unit (e.g., Feet) and draw on the map to calculate the length.

description: The More Options menu provides additional actions related to the current map or workspace.
You can find this option on the top-right corner of the map interface (three-dot icon).
Clicking this icon opens a dropdown menu containing options such as Pull Vector and Analysis.
From More Options click on Pull Vector to add a new vector layer to the map from the database.
Select dataset and click on Pull data
The Analysis Dashboard provides a visual report of the analysis performed within a workspace.
The dashboard can be accessed from any workspace.
Click on the 'Analysis' option from the workspace toolbar to open the Analysis Dashboard.
The Analytics Dashboard provides all the controls required to generate and view analysis reports.
Click the Download icon to export the generated report.
Multiple reports can be displayed on the dashboard simultaneously.
Multiple displayed reports can be exported together using the Download option.
Click the Layer icon to open the Analytics panel.
The Analytics panel lists all available Models, Assessments, and Map Annotation Layers for the current workspace.
The selected layer can then be displayed on the dashboard using the available actions.
Expand the required section (Models, Assessments, or Map Annotation Layers) from the Analytics panel.
Click the Eye icon beside a layer to display its analysis on the dashboard.
Click the Grid icon to enable Grid Analysis for the selected layer.
Enter the required cell size and click 'Create Grid'.
Click any grid cell to view analytics specific to that grid.
If Grid Analysis is not enabled, analytics are displayed for the entire layer.
The Analysis Report provides a visual and statistical summary of the selected analysis layer or grid.
The summary cards display information such as AOI, Total Storage, Training Images, and the analysis creation date.
The report includes a map preview, analytics visualization, summary cards, and an attribute table.
Analytics can be viewed as either a Pie Chart or a Line Chart.
The Attribute Table can display values by Area or Count.
Area values can be be viewed in Square Metres (sq. m), Hectares (ha), or Square Kilometres (sq. km).
Click the Download icon beside the Attribute Table to export the table data.

subdescription: Use the Search box to locate a specific Grid Number.
Click 'Filter Column' to apply filters to the attribute table.
Choose the comparison operator (Greater Than, Less Than, or Equals).
Enter the required value to filter the records.
Click 'Reset' to remove all applied filters.

description: The Notifications panel provides updates and alerts related to activities within the platform.
It is accessible from the top-right corner of the interface via the bell icon.
